speaker: Kael
subtitle: Im­pos­si­ble. The re­inforce­ments should have ar­rived hours ago.

speaker: Lira
subtitle: I never wanted any of this. But here we are.

speaker: System
subtitle: All sys­tems nominal. Re­group at ex­fil point Bravo in five min­utes.

speaker: Dr. Eryndal
subtitle: The de­com­pres­sion se­quence will take ap­prox­i­mate­ly four­teen min­utes to com­plete. Please re­main in your pres­su­ri­zed suit at all times. Any breach of the hull will trigger an au­to­mat­ic seal­ing pro­to­col and im­me­di­ate lock­down.

speaker: Commander Vael
subtitle: I've re­viewed your re­quest care­ful­ly, and the an­swer is no. The risk is far too great. I will not au­tho­rise an op­er­ation of this scale on the ba­sis of in­com­plete in­tel­li­gence. Come back when you have some­thing con­clu­sive.

speaker: Tutorial
subtitle: Use the left ana­logue stick to move and the right to look around. Press [A] to jump, [B] to sprint, and [X] to in­ter­act. Your cur­rent ob­jec­tive is marked on the com­pass. Open the pause menu at any time to re­view your in­ven­to­ry and mis­sion pa­ram­e­ters.

speaker: Kael
subtitle: I thought you were dead. Ev­ery­one thought you were dead — the re­port was clear, no sur­vi­vors. And yet here you stand. I have spent the last three days try­ing to un­der­stand what that means, and I find my­self with more ques­tions than an­swers.

speaker: Narrator
subtitle: The Foun­da­tion had been built on a lie — not the kind you can ra­tion­al­ise away, but a fun­da­men­tal de­cep­tion woven into ev­ery doc­u­ment, ev­ery cer­e­mo­ny, and ev­ery les­son they ever taught. Gen­er­ation after gen­er­ation had built their en­tire world on some­thing that never ex­ist­ed. And now, at last, the truth was sur­fa­cing — and with it, the weight of ev­ery­thing they had lost.

speaker: Archivist Deen
subtitle: The re­con­struc­tion of the sur­face maps con­firms what our sen­sors have been in­dic­at­ing for months. Be­yond the sev­enth par­al­lel, ter­ri­to­ries once thought un­inhab­ited now show clear signs of an­cient col­o­ni­za­tion. The struc­tures pre­date any­thing the Con­fe­der­a­tion has pre­vi­ous­ly cat­al­ogued by sev­er­al thou­sand years. We do not yet know who built them — or why they were aban­doned.

speaker: Dr. Solenne
subtitle: We are look­ing at a bi­olog­i­cal sig­na­ture un­like any­thing pre­vi­ous­ly doc­u­ment­ed in the sci­en­ti­fi­c lit­er­ature. The im­pli­ca­tions for our un­er­stand­ing of ex­tra­ter­res­tri­al life are ex­traor­di­na­ry — this single sam­ple ef­fec­tive­ly in­val­id­ates two cen­tu­ries of as­sumed sci­en­ti­fi­cal con­sensus. Ev­ery­thing we thought we knew about the or­igns of life in this sec­tor will need to be re­vis­ited.

speaker: Narrator
subtitle: Three years had passed since the Col­lapse. The cities that sur­vived were un­rec­og­nis­able — their sky­lines re­shaped by the quakes, their streets si­lent in ways that no lan­guage yet had words for. Sur­vi­vors spoke of those first weeks in whis­pers, if they spoke of them at all. Most had sim­ply learned to live along­side the emp­ti­ness the way you learn to live along­side grief.
